verbatim_text: So what I'm going to present here is evidence for an alternative view that beyond these heuristics, models also learn these structured internal representations, reminiscent of our earlier AI systems. And I'm going to argue that by adopting such a view, this is not only descriptively plausible, but also normatively useful for enabling systematic targeted interventions that are interpretable, controllable, and human accessible. OK, so to understand what we need out of the structured models that we're going to look inside neural networks and try to extract, I'm going to start by organizing the failures that we currently see into failures of two types. So there's going to be failures of coherence and there's going to be failures of updatability.
